data.title: Chuangxin Industries Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Dividend, Board Mandates
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited ( (HK:2788) ) has issued an update.

Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for April 23, 2026, in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025 and vote on a proposed final dividend of HK$0.77 per share. Investors will also decide on the re-election of eight retiring directors, the authorisation of the board to set directors’ pay, the re-appointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor, and a general mandate empowering the board to issue additional shares, which could provide future funding flexibility and affect capital structure.

The proposed dividend signals management’s confidence in the company’s 2025 performance and offers a tangible return to shareholders ahead of the new financial year. The comprehensive slate of director re-elections and the renewal of the share issuance mandate underscore continuity in governance and strategic optionality, developments that are likely to be closely watched by investors assessing the company’s future growth plans and governance stability.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2788) stock is a Buy with a HK$32.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:2788 Stock Forecast page.

More about Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited

Chuangxin Industries Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 02788. The group operates through various subsidiaries and is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical diversified corporate structure in Hong Kong’s capital market.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

An announcement from Hygieia Group Limited ( (HK:1650) ) is now available.

Hygieia Group Limited issued a clarification regarding its previously published annual results announcement for the year ended 31 December 2025, correcting an omission about the currency used for its proposed final dividend. The board confirmed that the final dividend of 0.001 per share for 2025, compared with 0.002 per share a year earlier, is denominated in Singapore dollars, and emphasized that all other information in the original results announcement remains unchanged.

The correction removes potential ambiguity for shareholders and investors about the payout currency, which is important for assessing the actual value of the dividend and comparing it with prior years. By promptly addressing the oversight and reaffirming that no other figures or disclosures have been altered, the company aims to maintain transparency and reinforce confidence in its financial reporting among stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1650) stock is a Buy with a HK$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hygieia Group Limited stock, see the HK:1650 Stock Forecast page.

Hygieia Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, with its board led by chairman Toh Eng Kui and comprising executive and independent non-executive directors based in Singapore and Hong Kong. The company operates in the Asian market, though the specific industry and core products or services were not detailed in the announcement.

data.title: Greentech Technology Deepens Probe After Auditor Complaint on Past Fund Flows
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Greentech Technology International Limited ( (HK:0195) ) just unveiled an update.

Greentech Technology International has issued a further update on its prolonged compliance challenges, which have led to delays in publishing its 2024 interim and annual results and its 2025 interim results, as well as a continuing suspension of trading in its shares. The company has been working under resumption guidance and has already conducted an independent forensic investigation and internal control review as part of efforts to address regulatory concerns.

In the latest development, Greentech disclosed details of an anonymous complaint lodged with its auditor, prompting the appointment of OCF as an independent investigator and now the engagement of an additional independent investigator. The allegations center on allegedly questionable fund flows at a former subsidiary between 2020 and 2021 and a purported payment to a former director from proceeds of a loan repayment, deepening scrutiny of the group’s past transactions and governance and potentially impacting investor confidence and the timetable for trading resumption.

More about Greentech Technology International Limited

Greentech Technology International Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through a group structure that has included various subsidiaries. The company is currently subject to trading suspension on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong due to delayed financial reporting and ongoing regulatory and investigative matters affecting its recent interim and annual results.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$382.5M

Sany Heavy Equipment International Holdings Co ( (HK:0631) ) has provided an update.

Sany Heavy Equipment International Holdings has approved a new grant of 18,112,183 restricted shares to 1,716 eligible participants under its 2019 Restricted Share Award Scheme, signaling continued use of equity incentives to retain and motivate staff. The first batch covers 17,464,856 shares for 1,697 grantees, including allocations to the chief executive officer and a subsidiary supervisor, with vesting scheduled in five tranches from April 2027 to March 2031, potentially aligning management and employee interests more closely with long-term shareholder value.

The vesting timetable spreads 20% of the batch 1 restricted shares over each year from 2027 through 2031, encouraging sustained performance and tenure within the group. By extending equity awards broadly beyond top executives to other employees of the group, the company is reinforcing a long-term incentive structure that may support operational stability and competitiveness in the heavy equipment market.

data.title: Weigang Environmental Technology Wins HKEX Waiver to Appoint New Joint Company Secretary
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Weigang Environmental Technology Holding Group Ltd. ( (HK:1845) ) has issued an update.

Weigang Environmental Technology Holding Group Ltd. has appointed Xu Anli as joint company secretary effective 1 April 2026, highlighting her decade of experience in corporate investment, governance, compliance, and IPO matters, as well as her tenure as the company’s assistant secretary since December 2022. The board argues that her deep familiarity with the group’s operations and disclosure practices will strengthen secretarial functions and support corporate governance, capital management, and investor relations.

Because Xu does not yet meet the formal company secretary qualifications under Hong Kong Listing Rule 3.28, Weigang obtained a three-year conditional waiver from the Stock Exchange, requiring that she be assisted by qualified joint secretary Ms. Pang and complete at least 15 hours of relevant training annually. The waiver, which can be revoked in case of material Listing Rules breaches, underscores both the exchange’s flexibility and the company’s dependence on Xu’s expertise to maintain regulatory compliance while its main business remains outside Hong Kong.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1845) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Weigang Environmental Technology Holding Group Ltd. stock, see the HK:1845 Stock Forecast page.

More about Weigang Environmental Technology Holding Group Ltd.

The group focuses on environmental technology solutions, where robust corporate governance and regulatory compliance are central to its capital markets presence and investor relations strategy.

Palasino Holdings Limited ( (HK:2536) ) has issued an announcement.

Palasino Holdings Limited has announced the composition of its board of directors effective 1 April 2026, confirming Pavel Maršík as the sole executive director and chief executive officer, alongside chairman Tan Sri Dato’ David Chiu and non-executive director Cheong Thard Hoong. The board also detailed the membership of its audit, nomination and remuneration committees, as well as leadership roles for its environmental, social and governance and investment committees, underscoring a reinforced governance framework and clear division of oversight responsibilities for shareholders and other stakeholders.

Independent non-executive directors Dr. Ngai Wing Liu, Mr. Kam Choi Rox Lam, Ms. Sin Kiu Ng, Ms. Jie Jiao and Ms. Chuchu Tracy Li will chair or serve on key governance committees, with Dr. Liu chairing both the audit and remuneration committees and Chiu chairing the nomination committee. Maršík will lead the environmental, social and governance committee and Hoong will chair the investment committee, highlighting the company’s emphasis on formalized board oversight in risk management, executive pay, board composition and sustainability-related matters.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2536) stock is a Hold with a HK$2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Palasino Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:2536 Stock Forecast page.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Everbright Water ( (SG:U9E) ) just unveiled an announcement.

China Everbright Water has secured two new wastewater treatment projects in Jiangsu Province, extending its footprint in the Yangtze River Delta. The company will invest about RMB90 million in Phase III of the Huai’an Huaiyin Eastern District Waste Water Treatment Project under a BOT model, adding 20,000 m3 of daily capacity and aligning the concession with existing phases through 2050.

It also won the EPC+O contract for Phase I Stage 2 of the Jiangyin High-tech Zone Waste Water Treatment Plant, further strengthening its portfolio of municipal water projects. These wins deepen Everbright Water’s cooperation with local governments, optimise its business mix in advanced industrial regions and reinforce its positioning as a leading regional water environment services provider.

More about China Everbright Water

China Everbright Water Limited is an environmental protection company specialising in water environment management across China, with shares listed in Hong Kong and Singapore. The company focuses on municipal wastewater treatment and related engineering, procurement, construction and operation services, with a growing presence in key economic regions such as the Yangtze River Delta.

An announcement from China Wah Yan Healthcare ( (HK:0648) ) is now available.

SoftMedx Healthcare Limited has completed the issuance of new scheme shares under a specific mandate, following the fulfillment of all conditions precedent on 1 April 2026. The transaction modestly increased the company’s total share capital to about 2.14 billion shares and introduced a trustee as a new public shareholder, slightly diluting the controlling stake of Multi Omniverse Group Limited from 74.9% to 74.4% while marginally broadening the public float.

The revised shareholding structure leaves the controlling shareholder’s influence effectively intact but signals a small step toward diversifying ownership through the scheme share arrangement. For minority investors, the change marginally enhances liquidity and public participation in the stock, although governance and strategic control remain firmly in the hands of the dominant shareholder group.

More about China Wah Yan Healthcare

SoftMedx Healthcare Limited, formerly known as China Wah Yan Healthcare, operates in the healthcare sector and is listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The company’s ownership is highly concentrated, with controlling shareholder Multi Omniverse Group Limited holding roughly three-quarters of its issued share capital, while the remainder is held by public investors, including a trustee structure and other minority shareholders.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

JD Logistics, Inc. ( (HK:2618) ) has issued an announcement.

JD Logistics has granted 4,250,355 new share awards under its Post-IPO Share Award Scheme to employees of the group, excluding directors and senior management, representing about 0.06% of its issued share capital as of the grant date. The awards, granted at a market price of HK$14.14 per share, vest in tranches over periods ranging from 0.25 to four years, with some shorter vesting linked to batch administration, and are designed to incentivize and retain staff without attaching specific performance targets.

The new grants incorporate detailed clawback provisions that allow the company to forfeit or cancel unvested and even vested shares, and reclaim related proceeds, in cases such as dismissals without notice, criminal convictions involving integrity or honesty, or conduct damaging to the group’s interests or reputation. These grants are also subject to broader clawback rules tied to potential financial restatements at JD Logistics or its parent, signaling a strong emphasis on compliance, governance, and alignment of employee interests with long-term shareholder value.

Geotech Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1707) ) has provided an update.

Geotech Holdings Ltd. has announced it will miss the 31 March 2026 deadline to publish its audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2025, after the resignation of its previous auditor and difficulties in appointing a new one amid U.S. sanctions on an individual believed to be a beneficial owner of a substantial shareholder. The board has decided not to issue unaudited management accounts, warned that the annual report may also be delayed in breach of listing requirements, and confirmed that trading in its shares will be suspended from 1 April 2026 until the 2025 results are released, prompting a cautionary notice to shareholders and potential investors.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Fullshare Holdings ( (HK:0607) ).

Fullshare Holdings has requested a suspension of trading in its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange from 9:00 a.m. on 1 April 2026, as the company will not be able to publish its audited financial results for the year ended 31 December 2025 on time. Its auditor, Prism Hong Kong Limited, expects to complete the audit of the FY2025 consolidated financial statements by the end of May 2026 at the earliest, delaying the release of the 2025 annual results and related report.

The company said it will issue further announcements to confirm the board meeting date to approve the results, the publication date of the 2025 annual results, and the dispatch date of the annual report. The suspension and disclosure timetable add uncertainty for shareholders and potential investors, who have been urged by the board to exercise caution when dealing in Fullshare’s shares while the audit work and regulatory disclosures remain pending.

More about Fullshare Holdings

Fullshare Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 00607. The group operates as an investment holding company with diversified interests, and its shares are held by a mix of institutional and retail investors in Hong Kong and abroad.

data.title: China Beidahuang Delays 2025 Results and Suspends Trading Amid Unpaid Audit Fees
data.description:

China Beidahuang Industry Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:0039) ) has shared an update.

China Beidahuang Industry Group Holdings Limited, a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 00039, announced changes affecting its financial reporting and trading status. The board, which includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, oversees the group’s compliance with listing and disclosure obligations in the Hong Kong market.

The company disclosed that it has not settled its outstanding audit fee for the year ended 31 December 2024, preventing its auditor from commencing the audit for the 2025 financial year. As a result, China Beidahuang will miss the 31 March 2026 deadline for publishing its 2025 annual results and has requested a suspension of trading in its shares from 1 April 2026, advising shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution while it works with its auditor to determine new reporting timelines.

The delay in audited results and the resulting trading suspension underscore heightened compliance and transparency risks for investors, as the company cannot yet confirm when its 2025 annual results and annual report will be available. The board has committed to issuing further announcements when appropriate, but the current uncertainty may weigh on market confidence until the audit and publication timetable is clarified.

China Beidahuang Industry Group Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, with a diversified board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors.
